summary: Frontend performance optimization testing completed. All 6 features verified working correctly: skeleton loading components, page preloading on sidebar hover, optimized PageLoadingSpinner, POS page, Employees page with memoized subcomponents, and smooth navigation transitions.

issues: Minor: Missing aria-describedby attribute on some dialog components (accessibility warning in console)

passed_tests: Login with superadmin credentials works correctly
Dashboard loads with all Quick Actions, Overview stats, and product catalogue
PageSkeleton component imported and used as fallback in PageLoadingSpinner
Sidebar navigation buttons have onMouseEnter to trigger preloadPage function
preloadPage function correctly preloads POS, Employees, Items, Inventory, Purchases, Sales, Customers, Suppliers pages
POS page loads and displays: header, search input, cart section, Quick Add, Pay button, store selector, quick links (Customers, Items, Vouchers, Purchases, Ledger)
Employees page loads with employee cards showing name, designation, store, contact info
Employees page displays tabs (Emp count, Apps count) and Add Employee button
EmployeesList.jsx memoized component renders employee cards correctly with Profile, ID Card, Upgrade, Edit, Delete buttons
Navigation between pages (Dashboard, POS, Items, Inventory, Customers, Employees) works smoothly
Items page loads with product cards, prices, stock status
Inventory page loads with stats cards and inventory table
Customers page loads with customer cards and loyalty info
Add Employee modal opens when clicking Add Employee button
Theme toggle button present and accessible
critical_code_review_comments: GOOD: PageSkeleton is properly imported from Skeletons.jsx and used in PageLoadingSpinner fallback
GOOD: preloadPage function uses React.useRef to track already preloaded pages, preventing duplicate preloads
GOOD: preloadMap covers heavy pages: pos, employees, items, inventory, purchases, sales, customers, suppliers
GOOD: All lazy-loaded pages use React.lazy with dynamic import for code splitting
GOOD: EmployeesList.jsx uses memo, useMemo, and useCallback for performance optimization
GOOD: EmployeeCard component is memoized with useMemo for store lookup
GOOD: Skeleton components have displayName set for React DevTools
GOOD: performanceOptimizer.js provides cachedFetch with TTL, deduplication, and preloading utilities
MINOR: Consider adding aria-describedby to DialogContent components for better accessibility
seed_data_creation: No seed data created - tested with existing data (5 employees, 21 items, 4 customers)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Performance optimization features are fully implemented and working. PageSkeleton is used as loading fallback, preloadPage function on sidebar hover preloads heavy pages, EmployeesList uses memoization. All navigation and interactions verified working.

skeleton_loading.implementation: PageSkeleton imported from Skeletons.jsx, used in PageLoadingSpinner as Suspense fallback

page_preloading.implementation: preloadPage function attached to onMouseEnter on sidebar nav buttons, preloads: pos, employees, items, inventory, purchases, sales, customers, suppliers

optimized_loading_spinner.implementation: PageLoadingSpinner now renders PageSkeleton instead of plain spinner

memoized_employees_list.implementation: EmployeesList.jsx uses memo, useMemo for filtered employees and stats, useCallback for search handler
